stories.title: Trump warns strikes will resume if Iran doesn't agree to his peace terms
stories.description: President Trump said that any peace deal would not allow nuclear enrichment in Iran, and would need to keep the Strait of Hormuz open, as conflicting messages surface over the terms of the ceasefire.

stories.title: Morning news brief
stories.description: Trump and NATO chief meet to discuss the partnership, attacks continue across the Gulf region despite U.S.-Iran ceasefire, Iran says ceasefire includes Lebanon, but Israel and Trump say it's not.

stories.title: What has the U.S. war with Iran accomplished?
stories.description: Trump's war goals included putting an end to Iran's nuclear program, destroying its military capabilities and creating regime change. That hasn't happened.

stories.title: Oil prices plunge and stocks soar after U.S. and Iran agree on a ceasefire
stories.description: Investors around the world breathed a sigh of relief at the prospect of peace — and an easing of the global energy crisis.

stories.title: U.S., Iran agree to 2-week ceasefire. And, Trump-backed Clay Fuller wins House race
stories.description: The U.S. reached a last-minute ceasefire with Iran just before Trump's deadline for the country to meet his demands. And, Trump-backed Clay Fuller wins the U.S. House race in Georgia.

stories.title: At least 182 killed across Lebanon in large wave of Israeli strikes
stories.description: Attacks hit the southern suburbs of Beirut, southern Lebanon and the eastern Bekaa Valley, hours after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced.

stories.title: Negotiators face huge task to close gaps in rival Iran peace proposals
stories.description: A US 15-point plan and an Iranian 10-point variant are oceans apart, writes the BBC's diplomatic correspondent.

stories.title: Israel backs US-Iran ceasefire but Netanyahu's war goals remain unfulfilled
stories.description: The Israeli prime minister's response to the ceasefire has been muted, in contrast to the triumphal statements from the US and Iran.

stories.title: Why ceasefire deal with US has unsettled Iran's hardliners
stories.description: The two-week truce opens up the prospect of direct talks with the US but has angered Iran's hardliners, writes BBC News Persian’s Kasra Naji.

stories.title: Iran Strait of Hormuz warning adds to shipping uncertainty
stories.description: Only a few vessels have crossed the strait since the US-Iran ceasefire deal, according to BBC Verify analysis.

stories.title: Iran ceasefire deal gives Trump a way out of war - but at a high cost
stories.description: The path to the two-week ceasefire with Iran may have fundamentally altered the way the rest of the world views the US.

stories.title: What we know about the two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran
stories.description: The provisional truce comes more than a month after the US and Israel launched coordinated attacks on Iran.

stories.title: How Pakistan helped secure a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran
stories.description: Pakistan has a historic relationship with Iran, but an agreement was far from certain, writes the BBC’s Caroline Davies

stories.title: Oil prices plunge and shares jump on US-Iran ceasefire plan
stories.description: Crude prices tumbled by as much as 15% on the conditional pause but remain far higher than before the war.

stories.title: French couple leave Iran after more than three years in jail
stories.description: Cecile Kohler and Jacques Paris are on their way home after being allowed to leave the country, the French president says.

stories.title: Trump condemned over threat that Iran's 'civilisation will die'
stories.description: The UN's chief says he is "deeply troubled" by such statements, as the US president ramps up pressure on Iran to agree a deal to end the war.

stories.title: Trump issues expletive-laden threat to Iran over Hormuz Strait blockage
stories.description: The US president says he will destroy Iranian power plants and bridges if the vital waterway is not reopened.

stories.title: Little sign of breakthrough as Trump's Iran deadline nears
stories.description: The US president is in a delicate political position as the final hours to Tuesday's deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz tick down.

stories.title: How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
stories.description: The operation to extract him from the ground in hostile territory was hugely complex and involved multiple US government agencies.

stories.title: Experts dispute US account of deadly Iran sports hall strike in Lamerd
stories.description: Six weapons experts have contested the US claim that video evidence suggests an Iranian missile could have hit the hall.

stories.title: Chart shows Iran may have put sea mines in Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Semiofficial news agencies in Iran published a chart Thursday suggesting the country’s paramilitary Revolutionary Guard put sea mines into the Strait of Hormuz during the war, in a message that may be intended to pressure the US as uncertainty hangs over a days-old two-week cease...

stories.title: US-Iran truce hangs in balance as war flares in Lebanon
stories.description: The fragile two-week truce between Iran and the United States was hanging in the balance on Wednesday, with Tehran threatening to resume hostilities as Israel launched a major bombardment of Lebanon. At least 182 people were killed and nearly 900 wounded on Wednesday, the Lebanes...

stories.title: US–Iran ceasefire: Has Iran’s regime emerged weakened or reinforced?
stories.description: US President Donald Trump declared the war a complete success following the ceasefire. Iran, despite significant damage to infrastructure and its military, also claimed victory. Even though weakened and demoralised, has the Iranian regime emerged strengthened from these 40 days o...

stories.title: Tehran residents welcome ceasefire but voice concerns
stories.description: Residents in the Iranian capital Tehran have welcomed the ceasefire agreement reached with the United States, but also voiced concerns over how long it might hold. After some 40 days of deadly clashes, the United States and Iran on Wednesday agreed to a two-week ceasefire, and co...

stories.title: Netanyahu faces mounting criticism as Israel’s war goals in Iran remain unmet
stories.description: For Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu's critics, Israel is seen as the biggest loser of the conflict with Iran. His war aims including regime change and an end to uranium enrichment have not yet been achieved. And international opinion of Israel has fallen further since the start of ...

stories.title: On Truth Social, die-hard MAGA supporters start to turn on Trump
stories.description: PRESS REVIEW – Thursday, April 10: We bring you world reactions after the US and Iran signed a fragile two-week ceasefire. Many papers condemn Donald Trump's claims of victory and highlight how once-fervent MAGA supporters are now openly criticising him on Truth Social. Also: a F...

stories.title: Pakistan plays major role trying to mediate an end to Iran war
stories.description: Pakistan has emerged as a key mediator between the US and Iran, playing a crucial role in brokering the two-week ceasefire agreement and working with both camps, as well as regional powers, to end the war. Islamabad has ties to Tehran and Washington and is set to hold talks this ...

stories.title: Netanyahu fails to address Israel’s failure to achieve war goals in Iran
stories.description: Reporting from Jerusalem, FRANCE 24’s Noga Tarnopolsky says that when Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu finally made a statement on the US-Iran ceasefire, he failed to “address the fact that Israel has not achieved any of its stated war goals, which included regime change in Iran...

stories.title: US-Iran ceasefire: Why Tehran wants to charge ships for crossing the Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: As part of its 10-point proposal to end the US-Israeli war on Iran, Tehran is pushing for ongoing control over the vital Strait of Hormuz – including charging what officials suggest could be as much as $2 million per ship to help the country rebuild its bombed-out infrastructure ...

stories.title: Middle East live: France blasts Israel’s ‘unacceptable’ Lebanon strikes, ceasefire hangs in balance
stories.description: French Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot on Thursday condemned Israel’s "unacceptable" strikes on Lebanon, which killed 182 people and wounded 890 the day after the US and Iran reached a two-week ceasefire agreement. As Iran and Hezbollah retaliated for the attacks, Barrot warned...

stories.title: Iran-US truce on brink as Israel pounds Lebanon, Tehran warns of escalation
stories.description: A fragile two-week truce between Iran and the United States hung in the balance on Wednesday as Tehran threatened renewed hostilities and Israel launched heavy strikes on Lebanon. Both sides claimed victory, but at least 182 people were killed and nearly 900 wounded, Lebanese aut...

stories.title: Trump meets NATO’s Rutte as US weighs exit over Iran war
stories.description: US President Donald Trump held talks with NATO chief Mark Rutte on Wednesday, the White House said, as he weighed leaving the alliance after it did not join the US in its war against Iran. The Wall Street Journal reported that Trump was also considering withdrawing US troops from...

stories.title: ‘Collective punishment’: At least 182 killed in Israeli strikes on Lebanon
stories.description: Lebanon’s health ministry says Israeli strikes on Wednesday killed 182 people – the deadliest single day since the start of the Israel–Hezbollah war. Israel launched a wave of strikes on central Beirut and other parts of the country, even as a fragile ceasefire between the United...

stories.title: 'Pakistani military calling the shots: Has connections with IRCG, very good relationship with Trump'
stories.description: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Ali Vaez, Director of Iran Project & Senior Advisor at the International Crisis Group. While the US sees the outcome as a decisive military victory, Vaez argues that the more consequential story lies in the reconfiguration of power within Iran. ...

stories.title: Energy crisis not over despite relief rally
stories.description: Oil prices have plunged and stock markets soared after the announcement of a two-week ceasefire between the US and Israel and Iran. But regardless of whether the deal holds, the energy crisis unleashed by the war is far from over. Damage to infrastructure in the region means it c...

stories.title: Several people reported killed in fresh Israeli attacks on Lebanon
stories.description: Lebanon bears the brunt and US-Iran truce at risk as Israel claims killing of a close aide to Hezbollah's leader.

stories.title: The war on Iran: Nobody won, everyone paid
stories.description: The US-Israeli campaign has failed to achieve its goals. Iran has been badly hit, and the Gulf is paying the bill too.

stories.title: Can global supply chains recover from the Iran war?
stories.description: Conflict upends flow of critical raw materials for manufacturing, aviation and technology.

stories.title: Iran has been bloodied, but it is winning against the US-Israel axis
stories.description: The US and Israel thought they could decimate Iran. But Iran's survival - despite massive human costs - is a victory.

stories.title: Trump says US forces to stay near Iran, ready for ‘next conquest’
stories.description: US threats against Iran and ongoing Israeli attacks in Lebanon cast doubt on fragile ceasefire efforts.

stories.title: Iran war day 41: What’s happening in Lebanon, Middle East and beyond?
stories.description: Lebanon has declared a national day of mourning after Israeli attacks killed at least 254 people in a single day.

stories.title: For Gulf states, Hormuz uncertainty casts shadow over US-Iran ceasefire
stories.description: Experts say Gulf countries are wary of the US agreeing to a deal that gives Iran leverage over the vital energy route.

stories.title: Trump slams NATO over Iran after meeting Rutte, renews Greenland threat
stories.description: US president meets NATO chief, expresses disappointment over member states failing to back war on Iran.

stories.title: US VP JD Vance says Lebanon is not part of the US-Iran ceasefire
stories.description: US Vice President JD Vance says Lebanon is not part of the US-Iran ceasefire.

stories.title: Iran war live: Trump threatens action if Tehran fails to comply with deal
stories.description: Trump says US assets to remain in Gulf until 'real deal' with Iran as US, Israel say Lebanon not included in ceasefire.

stories.title: ‘Israel must be restrained for US-Iran ceasefire to hold’
stories.description: Former US counterterrorism chief Joe Kent had warned Israel could ‘sabotage’ the US-Iran ceasefire.

stories.title: Americans question Trump’s instinct on Iran war
stories.description: Americans are split on whether to trust Donald Trump's instinct-driven approach to the Iran conflict.

stories.title: Iran, Saudi FMs hold first call since start of conflict, discuss ways to reduce tensions
stories.description: Saudi Foreign Minister Prince Faisal bin Farhan received a phone call from his Iranian counterpart Abbas Araghchi, the Saudi foreign ministry said on Thursday. It marked the first official contact between the countries since Tehran launched strikes against its Gulf neighbours in ...

stories.title: Pakistan condemns Israeli aggression against Lebanon in ‘strongest terms’, says it undermines peace efforts
stories.description: Pakistan on Thursday strongly condemned Israel’s continued attacks on Lebanon, cautioning that they undermine global efforts for regional peace. “Pakistan condemns, in the strongest terms, the ongoing Israeli aggression against Lebanon, resulting in the loss of innocent lives and...

stories.title: Pakistan’s envoy to US acknowledges China’s quiet but persuasive role in Iran talks
stories.description: WASHINGTON: Pakistan’s mediation that helped bring about a temporary US–Iran ceasefire has drawn global attention, with Islamabad’s envoy to Washington, Rizwan Saeed Sheikh, reiterating that the breakthrough was the result of “an intense diplomatic effort that was in progress ove...

stories.title: Foolproof security arranged for foreign guests, Naqvi assures US envoy ahead of US‑Iran talks in Islamabad
stories.description: Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi on Thursday assured US Ambassador to Pakistan Natalie Baker of foolproof security for all foreign dignitaries ahead of the US-Iran talks scheduled in Islamabad this week. Pakistan is set to host in-person talks between the US and Iran on Saturday af...

stories.title: Millions vote in Assam, Kerala as India begins series of state elections
stories.description: Millions of Indians queued to vote in local elections in the Assam and Kerala states on Thursday, kicking off four key contests this month amid a war in the Middle East that has triggered some fuel shortages, even though Prime Minister Narendra Modi has kept domestic prices in ch...

stories.title: Trump declares end to Iran’s uranium enrichment
stories.description: • Vows joint push to ‘dig up, remove’ buried nuclear material; threatens 50pc tariffs on nations supplying weapons to Tehran• Hegseth says US will ‘take’ uranium if Iran refuses to hand it over WASHINGTON: President Don­ald Trump said Wednesday there will be no enrichment of uran...

stories.title: Islamabad takes centre stage, to host high-stakes US-Iran talks
stories.description: • Negotiations follow Pakistan-brokered two-week truce• US vice president, Iranian foreign minister to lead their delegations• PM Shehbaz calls talks ‘historic moment’ for lasting settlement• Iran warns of withdrawal from ceasefire if violations continue• US says truce excludes L...

stories.title: 15 nations plan to help restore traffic through Hormuz: Macron
stories.description: French President Emmanuel Macron welcomes his counterpart from Ghana, John Dramani Mahama, to the Elysee Palace.—AFP • President says France to lead mission for securing key oil route after US-Iran truce• First ships cross strategic strait; major shipping lines hold back, citing ...

stories.title: US urged to end Ukraine war after Iran truce
stories.description: • Ukranian FM says ‘American decisiveness’ in Gulf can be replicated• Kremlin also signals hope for peace talks• US vice president calls conflict ‘hardest to solve’, pledges continued effort KYIV: Kyiv on Wednesday called on the United States to pressure Russia into ending its in...

stories.title: World Bank cuts Middle East growth forecast after energy sector turmoil
stories.description: DUBAI: The World Bank has slashed its 2026 growth forecast for Middle East economies as a consequence of the US-Israel war against Iran, and warned of pervasive risks in a report published on Wednesday. President Donald Tru­mp late on Tuesday announced a two-week ceasefire in the...

stories.title: Iran’s uranium enrichment secures independence, peaceful atom — supreme leader
stories.description: Ali Khamenei added that the US and other countries are not interested in Iran's progress and have put forward conditions presupposing a complete halt of uranium enrichment on Iranian territory

stories.title: Tehran could weigh nuclear deal with US with Iran-based enrichment consortium — Axios
stories.description: According to the report, a sixth round of talks on the Iranian nuclear program between the US president’s Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and Iran’s top diplomat, Abbas Araghchi, could be held this weekend in a Middle Eastern country

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
